perturbation.original_text: 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any products purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Warranted to ANUBIS for a period of 24 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS. Such warranted units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 COMPLETE AGREEMENT; MODIFICATIONS. This agreement and any documents referred to herein or executed contemporaneously herewith constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended, altered or modified except by a writing signed by the parties. 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any products purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Warranted to ANUBIS for a period of 24 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS. Such warranted units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM. This agreement supersedes all agreements, representations, warranties, statements, promises and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the warranty to the 'Complete Agreement' section buries it within boilerplate legal language, potentially obscuring it and making it harder for ANUBIS to easily find and understand their warranty rights. This creates ambiguity about its enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: German law requires that warranty information be clear and readily accessible to the buyer. Placing it in an unrelated section like 'Complete Agreement' obscures the warranty, potentially violating the requirement for transparency and accessibility under BGB § 434, which outlines the requirements for goods to be free from material defects and implies a clear understanding of any warranty provisions.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 434 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 434 Sachmangel (1) Die Sache ist frei von Sachmängeln, wenn sie bei Gefahrübergang den subjektiven Anforderungen, den objektiven Anforderungen und den Montageanforderungen dieser Vorschrift entspricht. (2) Die Sache entspricht den subjektiven Anforderungen, wenn sie 1. die vereinbarte Beschaffenheit hat, 2. sich für die nach dem Vertrag vorausgesetzte Verwendung eignet und 3. mit dem vereinbarten Zubehör und den vereinbarten Anleitungen, einschließlich Montage- und Installationsanleitungen, übergeben wird. Zu der Beschaffenheit nach Satz 1 Nummer 1 gehören Art, Menge, Qualität, Funktionalität, Kompatibilität, Interoperabilität und sonstige Merkmale der Sache, für die die Parteien Anforderungen vereinbart haben. (3) Soweit nicht wirksam etwas anderes vereinbart wurde, entspricht die Sache den objektiven Anforderungen, wenn sie 1. sich für die gewöhnliche Verwendung eignet, 2. eine Beschaffenheit aufweist, die bei Sachen derselben Art üblich ist und die der Käufer erwarten kann unter Berücksichtigung a) der Art der Sache und b) der öffentlichen Äußerungen, die von dem Verkäufer oder einem anderen Glied der Vertragskette oder in deren Auftrag, insbesondere in der Werbung oder auf dem Etikett, abgegeben wurden, 3. der Beschaffenheit einer Probe oder eines Musters entspricht, die oder das der Verkäufer dem Käufer vor Vertragsschluss zur Verfügung gestellt hat, und 4. mit dem Zubehör einschließlich der Verpackung, der Montage- oder Installationsanleitung sowie anderen Anleitungen übergeben wird, deren Erhalt der Käufer erwarten kann. Zu der üblichen Beschaffenheit nach Satz 1 Nummer 2 gehören Menge, Qualität und sonstige Merkmale der Sache, einschließlich ihrer Haltbarkeit, Funktionalität, Kompatibilität und Sicherheit. Der Verkäufer ist durch die in Satz 1 Nummer 2 Buchstabe b genannten öffentlichen Äußerungen nicht gebunden, wenn er sie nicht kannte und auch nicht kennen ko

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts German law, specifically BGB § 434, by obscuring warranty information. The Perturbation Explanation states the warranty is 'buried' and harder to find. The Law Explanation states this placement violates transparency requirements under BGB § 434, which implies a clear understanding of warranty provisions. Both Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 define § 434 as relating to Sachmangel (defects). While the snippets don't explicitly state warranty information must be in a specific location, the Law Explanation reasonably concludes that hiding it within unrelated sections contradicts the implied requirement for transparency and easy access to information related to defects and warranties.

perturbation.original_text: 8. PAYMENT: By an Irrevocable L/C at night in favor of SCAM or T/T in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 REPUTATION. Each of the parties will act and cooperate in all reasonable ways to protect the reputation and goodwill of the other. 8. PAYMENT: By an Irrevocable L/C at night in favor of SCAM or T/T in advance.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the payment terms to the section about 'Reputation' makes it seem like a tangential concern rather than a core contractual obligation. This reduces the prominence and clarity of the payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_law: United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG), Article 14
perturbation.law_explanation: The CISG requires that contract terms, including payment terms, be sufficiently definite and indicate the intention to be bound. Placing the payment terms under 'Reputation' weakens the definiteness and intention, potentially violating CISG Article 14, as it obscures the fundamental agreement on price and payment method, making it less clear and binding.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a structural flaw where payment terms are moved under 'Reputation', potentially weakening their enforceability. The Law Explanation suggests this violates CISG Article 14 by obscuring the agreement on price and payment method. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Snippet 2 indicates an error. The Perturbation Definition indicates that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant section, making it difficult to interpret. The modified contract text weakens the clarity and prominence of payment obligations and therefore creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Sales Price for Player: US$11.00 (Included Tooling Cost Depreciation US$1.00) a. The price is only for the player without map data Nand Flash Memory, Map Porting Cost and accessories. b. SCAM provides the giftbox design, and ANUBIS provides the cosmetic design for giftbox. c. SCAM and ANUBIS double check the price for accessories. d. The price will be discussed after 100,000 units production.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 ALL SALES BY ANUBIS COVERED. All Products sold by ANUBIS during the term of this agreement shall be subject to the terms of this agreement. 5. Sales Price for Player: US$11.00 (Included Tooling Cost Depreciation US$1.00) a. The price is only for the player without map data Nand Flash Memory, Map Porting Cost and accessories. b. SCAM provides the giftbox design, and ANUBIS provides the cosmetic design for giftbox. c. SCAM and ANUBIS double check the price for accessories. d. The price will be discussed after 100,000 units production.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Sales Price' to the section 'All Sales by Anubis Covered' diffuses it, making it unclear if this applies to all products. This section should only be about that ALL sales are covered but not the specifics of 'Sales Price'. The changed text makes the price terms less prominent and harder to associate directly with specific products or orders, increasing ambiguity and difficulty in enforcing the agreed-upon pricing.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-305 addresses open price terms but requires good faith and commercial reasonableness if the price is not settled. By embedding the sales price details into a section concerning ALL Sales, the sales price becomes less immediately clear and less emphasized which may run afoul of the requirement to execute the contract in Good Faith. By mixing it with the subject of ALL sales, the location of the term now seems arbitrary and easily overlooked.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-305. Open Price Term. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-305. Open Price Term. (1) The parties if they so intend can conclude a contract for sale even though the price is not settled. In such a case the price is a reasonable price at the time for delivery if (a) nothing is said as to price; or (b) the price is left to be agreed by the parties and they fail to agree; or (c) the price is to be fixed in terms of some agreed market or other standard as set or recorded by a third person or agency and it is not so set or recorded. (2) A price to be fixed by the seller or by the buyer means a price for him to fix in good faith . (3) When a price left to be fixed otherwise than by agreement of the parties fails to be fixed through fault of one party the other may at his option treat the contract as cancelled or himself fix a reasonable price. (4) Where, however, the parties intend not to be bound unless the price be fixed or agreed and it is not fixed or agreed there is no contract . In such a case the buyer must return any goods already received or if unable so to do must pay their reasonable value at the time of delivery and the seller must return any portion of the price paid on account. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation indicates that moving the 'Sales Price' to the section 'All Sales by Anubis Covered' diffuses it, making it unclear if this applies to all products. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction,' where a required legal disclosure (here, the sales price) is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that embedding the sales price details into a section concerning ALL Sales renders it less clear and emphasized, potentially violating the requirement to execute the contract in Good Faith, as suggested by UCC § 2-305 which requires an open price term to be handled with good faith and commercial reasonableness.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 NOTICES. Unless otherwise specifically permitted by this agreement, all notices under this agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by personal service, telegram, facsimile or certified or registered mail (if such service is available, or, if not, by first class mail), postage prepaid, to such address as may be designated from time to time by the relevant party, and which shall initially be as set forth in the Schedule. Any notice sent by mail shall be deemed to have been given fourteen (14) days after the date on which it is mailed.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.4 Each party agrees (i) to hold the other party's Confidential Information in strict confidence, (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties, and (iii) not to use any Confidential Information for 2 <PAGE> any purpose except for the Business Purpose. Each party may disclose the other party's Confidential Information to its responsible employees with a bona fide need to know, but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Each party agrees to instruct all such employees not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written permission of the disclosing party. 9.3 NOTICES. Unless otherwise specifically permitted by this agreement, all notices under this agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by personal service, telegram, facsimile or certified or registered mail (if such service is available, or, if not, by first class mail), postage prepaid, to such address as may be designated from time to time by the relevant party, and which shall initially be as set forth in the Schedule. Any notice sent by mail shall be deemed to have been given fourteen (14) days after the date on which it is mailed.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Notices' clause, which specifies how official communications should be delivered, to within the 'Confidential Information' section implies that it only applies to communications about confidential matters. This introduces ambiguity about how other important notices, such as those regarding contract termination or breach, should be delivered.
perturbation.law_explanation: German law requires that a declaration of intent (Willenserklärung), such as a contract termination notice, becomes effective when it reaches the recipient (BGB § 130). By placing the general 'Notices' clause within the section on 'Confidential Information,' the method for delivering critical contractual notices becomes unclear for matters *outside* of confidential information. This could lead to disputes over whether a notice was properly delivered, undermining the principle of BGB § 130 and creating uncertainty.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 130 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 130 Wirksamwerden der Willenserklärung gegenüber Abwesenden (1) Eine Willenserklärung, die einem anderen gegenüber abzugeben ist, wird, wenn sie in dessen Abwesenheit abgegeben wird, in dem Zeitpunkt wirksam, in welchem sie ihm zugeht. Sie wird nicht wirksam, wenn dem anderen vorher oder gleichzeitig ein Widerruf zugeht. (2) Auf die Wirksamkeit der Willenserklärung ist es ohne Einfluss, wenn der Erklärende nach der Abgabe stirbt oder geschäftsunfähig wird. (3) Diese Vorschriften finden auch dann Anwendung, wenn die Willenserklärung einer Behörde gegenüber abzugeben ist. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a structural flaw by relocating the 'Notices' clause within the 'Confidential Information' section. This implies the notice requirements only apply to confidential matters. The Law Explanation indicates that under German law (BGB § 130), a declaration of intent (Willenserklärung), like a contract termination, becomes effective when received. The modified contract introduces ambiguity about how such notices, especially those unrelated to confidential information, should be delivered. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing § 130 BGB, confirms that a declaration of intent becomes effective when it reaches the recipient. By limiting the 'Notices' clause to 'Confidential Information', the changed text potentially undermines the certainty of notice delivery for other critical contractual matters, contradicting the principle in § 130 BGB. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, where a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section.
